description:
You are an urban planner analyzing the polycentric employment structure of San Francisco using the UrbanSim dataset at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Aggregate jobs to zones using the jobs, buildings, and parcels tables, joining on building_id and parcel_id while using table indices as IDs and dropping any rows with missing zone_id values. Identify employment sub-centers as zones where employment density (total employment per parcel) is greater than the mean plus one standard deviation. Fit Zipf's law to the sub-center hierarchy using an OLS regression of log(rank) on log(total_employment) to determine the Zipf exponent. Document the entire analysis in a fully executed Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/polycentric_analysis.ipynb using the UrbanSim (Python 3) kernel.

Required outputs:
- A log-log scatter plot of rank vs total employment for sub-centers with the regression line at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/ranksize_plot.png.
- Zone employment data at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_employment_density.csv including zone_id, total_employment, num_parcels, employment_density, and is_subcenter (using 'True'/'False' strings).
- Sub-center rankings at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/subcenter_ranking.csv (sorted by rank).
- Regression statistics (zipf_exponent, r_squared, num_subcenters, total_citywide_employment, and density_threshold) at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zipf_results.json.
